name: ¡La Fantoma!
description: An issue of a pre-War comic book published by Hubris Comics, detailing the adventures of ¡La Fantoma!, a master of stealth and infiltration who uses cunning and agility to outwit enemies.
perk: When you succeed at a Sneak test to avoid notice, you may spend 1 AP to create a distraction somewhere within Medium range; the character who failed the opposed test to detect you heads towards the distraction you created. If you learn this perk, you may use it once per scene.
longDescription: An issue of a pre-War comic book, detailing the adventures of ¡La Fantoma!, a master of stealth and infiltration.

name: Astoundingly Awesome Tales
description: A pre-War pulp magazine containing thrilling short stories of daring adventure! Each issue features tales of heroism, monsters, and action that captivated readers before the Great War.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, granting bonus damage or bonus damage resistance against a single foe or type of attack. This lasts only for a single attack (made or received, respectively), unless the perk is learned, in which case it applies to all attacks.

issues.effect: +1 CD on attacks vs mirelurk
+1 CD on attacks vs super mutants
+2 to Physical and Energy damage resistances vs Robots
+2 to Poison damage resistance
+1 CD on attacks with Accurate weapons
+1 CD on attacks vs Ghouls
+2 to Radiation damage resistance
+1 CD on attacks with Energy weapons
+2 to Energy damage resistance
+1 CD on attacks where you spend one or more Ammunition for bonus damage

longDescription: A magazine containing thrilling short stories of daring adventure!

name: Backwoodsman
description: A pre-War magazine series containing articles and stories about outdoor survival, hunting, fishing, and living off the land.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.effect: Find twice as much meat from a butchered creature
Add +2 CD damage to a Thrown weapon attack (other than an Explosive)
Reduce the difficulty of crafting a weapon by 1 (min. of 0)
When scavenging from plants, gain twice as much
Add +2 CD damage to an attack against a Mammal or Lizard
One item of cooked food you make and eat heals +3 HP
Reduce difficulty of a test to resist catching a disease by 1 (min. of 0)
When you find ammo, you find +2 CD more shots
Reduce the difficulty of repairing a damaged item by 1 (min. 0)
Heal +2 HP when you consume an Alcoholic drink

longDescription: Thrilling, true stories for rugged men! Backwoodsman is a magazine series containing articles and stories about outdoor survival.

name: Boxing Times
description: A pre-War magazine aimed at fans of boxing, containing features on noteworthy boxers, discussions of technique and equipment, and in-depth reporting on the sport of boxing.
perk: Once, when you succeed at an Unarmed attack, if you spend any AP to increase the damage you dealt, you may add the Stun damage effect to your attack. If you later learn this perk, you may use this on any successful Unarmed attack.
longDescription: A pre-War magazine aimed at fans of boxing, often containing features on noteworthy or up-and-coming boxers, discussions of technique and equipment, and in-depth reporting on the sport.

name: Duck and Cover!
description: A pre-War instructional publication about the manufacture, use, and disarming of explosives.
perk: Once, when you are affected by a Blast weapon, you may choose to fall prone. If you do so, you add +3 to all damage resistances against the damage caused by that Blast. If you later learn this perk, you may use whenever you are affected by a Blast weapon by spending 1 AP.
longDescription: A pre-War book about the manufacture, use, and disarming of explosives.

name: Fixin' Things
description: A pre-War magazine for those dedicated to fixing things themselves.
perk: Once, when you attempt to repair an item, halve the amounts of the materials needed to complete the repair (round up). If you later learn this perk, you may use this whenever you attempt repairs.
longDescription: A magazine for those dedicated to fixing things themselves, each issue contains a wealth of information on how to mend and repair a variety of objects and materials.

name: Future Weapons Today
description: A pre-War magazine for enthusiasts of energy weaponry, featuring articles on new technologies, reviews of new weapons, and true stories about energy weapons in action on the battlefield.
perk: Once, when you make an attack with an Energy Weapon, you may overcharge it. Increase the damage by +2 CD for that attack. The weapon cannot be used to make an attack in the turn after an overcharged attack as it cools down. If you later learn this perk, you may overcharge an Energy Weapon once per scene.
longDescription: A magazine for enthusiasts of energy weaponry, featuring articles on new technologies, reviews of new weapons, and true stories about energy weapons in action.

name: Grognak the Barbarian
description: A series of comic books published by Hubris Comics before the Great War.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.effect: One successful melee attack you make inflicts +2 CD damage
Add +1 to the difficulty of another character's skill test to detect you
When you suffer Poison damage, gain +3 Poison damage resistance

issues.issue: In the Bosom of the Corsair Queen
issues.effect: One successful melee attack you make automatically inflicts a Critical Hit
One attack against Ghouls inflicts +2 CD damage

issues.issue: Enter Maula: War Maiden of Mars
issues.effect: When you make a melee attack with a Two-Handed melee weapon, gain the Piercing 1 damage effect (or +1 to any existing Piercing effect)
Ignore one complication on a melee attack

issues.issue: Lost in the Snows of Lust
issues.effect: Gain +2 Physical damage resistance against one attack

issues.issue: The Lair of the Virgin Eaters
issues.effect: Gain +10 Carry weight for one scene
Gain +2 Energy damage resistance against one attack

longDescription: A series of comic books published by Hubris Comics before the Great War. The comics depict the action-packed adventures of the title character in a prehistoric world.

name: Guns and Bullets
description: A pre-War magazine series catering to weapons enthusiasts, with articles dedicated to hunting, firearms technology, gun law, and the practical use of weapons in the field.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.effect: One attack against Robots inflicts +2 CD damage
One attack with a Laser weapon gains the Vicious damage effect if it didn't already have it
One attack with a Small Guns weapon gains the Vicious damage effect if it didn't have it already
Gain an extra 2 AP after one successful attack. This AP must be spent immediately
When you salvage a weapon, gain 2 AP which may only be spent to salvage additional units of materials

issues.issue: The Moon: A Communist Doomsday Device?!
issues.effect: Gain +2 to Physical or Energy damage resistance against one attack at night
One aimed attack made with a weapon which lacks the Accurate quality inflicts +2 CD damage
One attack against Yao Guai inflicts +3 CD damage
One attack with a Plasma weapon gains the Vicious damage effect if it didn't already have it
One attack against an enemy with a lower level than you inflicts +2 CD damage

longDescription: A magazine series catering to weapons enthusiasts, with articles dedicated to hunting, weapons, gun law, and the portrayal of practical use of firearms.

name: Live & Love
description: A pre-War magazine series dedicated to lifestyle and relationships, covering cultural events, health advice, diet tips, and relationship advice for the modern American.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from for a single scene. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit constantly.

issues.effect: +1 Maximum Health Points to all party members for one scene
+1 CD damage to attacks from all party members for one scene
Regain twice as many HP from fruit or vegetables eaten in one scene
At the start of one scene, add +1 AP to the group pool
Gain 1 Luck point (lost at the end of the scene if not used) when you consume an Alcoholic drink
+1 Physical damage resistance to all party members for one scene
The group AP pool may contain 1 AP more than normal for one scene
Choose one other Magazine perk you have previously used but not learned; that now takes effect
+2 to all damage resistances against Robots for one scene
Roll again on this table

longDescription: A pre-War magazine series dedicated to lifestyle and relationships, often covering cultural events, health advice, diet tips, relationship advice, and more.

name: Massachusetts Surgical Journal
description: A periodical medical journal from pre-War Massachusetts containing detailed discussions of advances in medical science and technology, published studies on unusual diseases, and useful medical knowledge.
perk: Once, when you provide medical attention to another character and assist their END + Survival test to heal Injuries, you may treat your assistance die as if it had rolled a 1. If you learn this perk, you may do this once per session.
longDescription: A periodical medical journal originating from pre-War Massachusetts. It contains detailed discussions of advances in medical science and technology, published studies on unusual diseases, and a host of other useful medical knowledge.

name: Meeting People
description: A pre-War magazine aimed at those who lack social grace, advertising itself as a primer for social interaction.
perk: Once, when you roll one or more complications on a Speech test, you may ignore those complications. If you learn this perk, you may do this once per scene.
longDescription: A magazine aimed at those who lack social grace. Meeting People magazine advertises itself as a primer for social interaction.

name: Programmer's Digest
description: A pre-War magazine for computer aficionados, discussing programming languages, encryption and decryption techniques, and the latest software developments and technologies.
perk: Once, when you fail a Science test to hack into a computer terminal and would be locked out, you may choose not to be locked out, allowing you to make another attempt. If you learn this Perk, you may do this whenever you are locked out of a computer.
longDescription: A magazine aimed at computer afficionados, discussing programming languages, encryption and decryption techniques, and the latest software.

name: Tales of a Junktown Jerky Vendor
description: A post-War collection of short, hand-made books written by an unknown vendor of jerky from Junktown, containing his observations, recipes, and tales from the wasteland.
perk: Once, when you succeed or fail at a Barter test to haggle on the price of a trade, you may spend 1 Luck point to shift the price 10% in your favor. If you learn this Perk, you may do this on any Barter test to haggle, though you must spend one additional Luck point each extra time you use it during a session (1 for the first time, 2 for the second, 3 for the third, etc.).
longDescription: One example of a post-War magazine, Tales of a Junktown Jerky Vendor is a collection of short, hand-made books written by an unknown vendor of jerky from Junktown.

name: Tesla Science Magazine
description: A pre-War informational magazine covering rocket science, robotics, laser technology, and other cutting-edge fields of science and technology.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.effect: +2 to Physical and Energy damage resistances vs Robots
+2 to Physical and Energy damage resistances vs Plasma weapons
+2 CD damage inflicted by one attack with a Blast weapon

issues.issue: Tomorrow's Technology for Today's Super Soldiers
issues.effect: After spending a fusion core charge, you may spend 1 Luck point: if you do so the charge is not spent
Once, when you fire a Gatling weapon, you consume ammunition at 8x the rate, rather than 10x the rate
+2 CD damage inflicted on one attack against a Mutated creature
On a single attack with an Energy weapon, you inflict a Critical Hit if you inflict 3 or more damage after damage resistance

issues.issue: 10 Number 1 Hits!!! Rock-o-bot Takes the Nation by Storm!!
issues.effect: Once, when you inflict a Critical Hit, increase the total damage inflicted by +2
On a single attack with a Big Gun, you inflict a Critical Hit if you inflict 3 or more damage after damage resistance
Roll again on this table

longDescription: An informational magazine covering topics such as rocket science, robotics, laser technology, and other cutting-edge fields of science and technology.

name: True Police Stories
description: A pre-War pulp magazine dedicated to editorialized stories on famous criminal investigations and police busts of the time, boasting thrilling tales of law enforcement.
perk: Once, when you roll the damage of an attack, you may spend a Luck point to turn up to three CD to any face, rather than rolling them. If you learn this perk, you may do this once per scene.
longDescription: True Police Stories was a pre-War pulp magazine boasting '20 pages—a single issue' that was dedicated to editorialized stories on famous criminal investigations and busts of the time.

name: Tumblers Today
description: A pre-War magazine containing information about the clandestine art of lockpicking and the latest in mechanical locks found in safes, footlockers, and doors.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.issue: Mysteries of the Master Key Exposed!
issues.effect: You may re-roll 1d20 on a single Lockpicking test

issues.issue: Bobby Pins: More Effective Than Lockpicks?
issues.effect: When using a Bobby Pin, reduce the difficulty of a Lockpicking test by a further 1 (minimum 0)
When scavenging or looting, spend 1 Luck to find 2+2 CD Bobby Pins

issues.issue: Open Any Lock in 5 Seconds Flat
issues.effect: When you successfully pick a lock, you may use the Reduce Time AP option without spending AP

issues.issue: Locksmith Certification Special—Pass with Flying Colors
issues.effect: When you suffer one or more complications on a Lockpicking test, spend 1 Luck point to ignore those complications

longDescription: Tumblers Today is a pre-War magazine containing information about the clandestine art of lockpicking and the latest in mechanical locks found in safes, footlockers, and doors.

name: Unstoppables
description: A pre-War comic book series from Hubris Comics following an ensemble cast of heroes including the Silver Shroud, Mistress of Mystery, The Inspector, Manta Man, and Grognak the Barbarian.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, allowing you to spend Luck points to avoid a single instance of damage, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit as often as you have Luck points to spend on it.

issues.issue: Dr. Brainwash and His Army of De-Capitalists!
issues.effect: Spend 3 Luck points to avoid all damage from a single attack or hazard

issues.issue: Who Can Stop the Unstoppable Grog-Na-Rok?!
issues.effect: Spend 2 Luck points to avoid all damage from an attack from a Mutated Human (including Ghouls and super mutants)
Spend 1 Luck point to avoid all damage from a Blast weapon

issues.issue: Trapped in the Dimension of the Pterror-dactyls!
issues.effect: Spend 1 Luck point to avoid all damage from a single melee attack
Spend 1 Luck point to avoid all Energy damage from a single attack

longDescription: Unstoppables is a comic book published by Hubris Comics prior to the Great War. The series follows an ensemble cast of characters including the Silver Shroud, Mistress of Mystery, The Inspector, Manta Man and Grognak the Barbarian.

name: U.S. Covert Operations Manual
description: A pre-War training handbook produced by the U.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.effect: If you are detected while sneaking, you add +2 to Physical damage resistance against the first attack you receive after being detected
Enemies suffer +1 difficulty on tests to detect you; this does not stack with any difficulty increases for poor light or darkness
+1 to all damage resistances against attacks from Character NPCs

issues.issue: FH 5-04 Not the Soldiers You're Looking For
issues.effect: +1 CD damage inflicted by attacks against Character NPCs
When you spend a Luck point to re-roll a d20 on a PER test, instead count that die as if it had rolled a 1
Once, when you suffer one or more Complications on a Sneak test, ignore the complications

issues.issue: FH 5-07 Getting the Drop on the Communists
issues.effect: Once, when an enemy aims before making an attack against you, you may cause them to lose the benefits of aiming

issues.issue: FH 5-08 Bushes, Boxes, and Beehives: Camouflage Special
issues.effect: +2 CD damage inflicted by an unarmed attack or an attack made with a knife
Once, when using a Stealth Boy, it lasts for one additional turn
When you spend a Luck point to re-roll a d20 on an AGI test, instead count that die as if it had rolled a 1

longDescription: The U.S. Covert Operations Manual is a training handbook produced by the Department of the Army to train its personnel in the art of subterfuge.

name: Wasteland Survival Guide
description: A post-War series of instructional magazines written and illustrated with crude pen drawings, offering practical advice for surviving the harsh realities of life in the wasteland.
perk: Depending on the individual issue found, you receive a different perk, which you may benefit from once. If you later learn the perk, you may use that benefit once per scene.

issues.effect: Consuming a fruit or vegetable food item heals twice as many HP
Increase all damage resistances by +2 vs an attack from an Insect

issues.issue: The Bright Side of Radiation Poisoning
issues.effect: When you consume irradiated food or drink, heal additional HP equal to the roll on the CD
When buying food or drink items, the price is shifted an additional 10% in your favor
Reduce the difficulty of an Athletics test to swim by 1 (min. 0)
Gain +1 defense against a melee attack
When you butcher an animal you've killed and the amount of meat found can be increased by spending AP, you gain 1 bonus AP for that purpose

longDescription: The Wasteland Survival Guide is another post-War series of instructional magazines, written and illustrated with crude pen drawings.
